description_long: This fence is constructed from thick, uneven wooden logs arranged horizontally and bound together with metal brackets and straps. The wood appears roughly hewn, with visible grain and irregular edges, giving it a rustic, hand-built appearance. The structure consists of several horizontal rails, with two prominent side posts and additional cross-bracing for stability. Metal elements, including brackets and straps, reinforce the joints and add to the utilitarian look. The top edge of the fence is flat, following the natural contours of the logs. There are no decorative elements or post caps, emphasizing a functional, rugged design suitable for temporary or makeshift barriers in urban or park settings.
description: A rustic, hand-built wooden fence made of thick, uneven logs bound with metal brackets and straps, featuring a flat top and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the horizontal wooden rails, metal brackets, and the flat top edge of the fence.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the cross-bracing and the way the logs are joined, with visible metal reinforcements.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to the front, showing the horizontal rails and metal straps from a different angle.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the cross-bracing and the attachment of the rails to the side posts.
